title: Former Oswego mayor to take over as top aide to Ryan McMahon
summary: Bill Barlow, former mayor of Oswego, has been appointed to a top position in Onondaga County government as a senior aide to County Executive Ryan McMahon, replacing Deputy County Executive Bob Petrovich. This leadership transition at the county level may have implications for city-county coordination on digital services, infrastructure projects, and intergovernmental initiatives.

political_signal.description: County executive appointment of experienced local government leader signals continuity in county leadership and potential focus on regional governance coordination.

processing_notes: Article focuses on county-level personnel change; limited direct relevance to City of Syracuse unless Barlow's role impacts city services or intergovernmental projects
No department, budget, or policy details provided in article
Context about Barlow's prior role and qualifications unavailable in article text
